checks.expected_outcome: queue mentions Top 3 system_id as a link, not a re-ranked recommendation
checks.implementation: buildLeverageQueueFromRoadmap accepts topThreeSystemIds and only adds linked_top3_system_id when one of the bundle's steps mentions it; queue does not produce its own ranking
checks.test_pinning: __tests__/lib/topThreeRecommendations.test.ts: links queue card to a top-3 system when one of its steps mentions it

checks.expected_outcome: bundle_id (e.g. TLS-BND-01) remains a roadmap label, never a graph node
checks.implementation: Queue UI renders bundle_id as a string; graph builder rejects it via registry contract
checks.test_pinning: __tests__/lib/topThreeRecommendations.test.ts: does not invent systems even when bundle id resembles one

checks.expected_outcome: queue card shows dependency_count from the roadmap entry
checks.implementation: queue card carries dependency_count derived from entry.dependencies length
checks.test_pinning: __tests__/lib/topThreeRecommendations.test.ts: compresses each bundle into a single card with dependency_count

checks.expected_outcome: queue card is one short summary; full details remain in Roadmap tab
checks.implementation: QueueItem replaced multi-line steps render with single-line bundle_id + title + why + deps + next_safe_action; full steps remain on RoadmapEntry payload accessible from Roadmap tab

checks.expected_outcome: queue card text uses 'next safe action' / 'why it matters' phrasing only — no closure / promotion / enforcement language
checks.implementation: labels rendered as 'why it matters' and 'next safe action'; no authority verbs
buildLeverageQueueFromRoadmap returns warning='Leverage queue unavailable: ...' when artifact missing

fix_summary: Compressed queue cards link to Top 3 systems by ID without introducing graph nodes; missing artifact surfaces fail-closed warning.
